section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports a headache and dizziness.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a patient-reported symptom history, not an exam or objective finding.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports flushing in his ears.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a subjective symptom reported by the patient, not an observed or measured finding.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports pain in the left hand.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a subjective complaint and belongs in the symptom history rather than the objective findings section.
A denial of a symptom is part of the review of symptoms/history, not an exam or test finding.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has a history of headaches and reports experiencing stress.
section_miscategorizations.reason: History of headaches and reported stress are historical/subjective details; while they inform assessment, they are not themselves a diagnostic impression.
This is reassurance/counseling language; it is acceptable in Plan but is non-specific and not a concrete plan element.
